item.title: Teaching
item.description: As an Instructor STATS 302 - Qualifying Exams Workshop (Theoretical Statistics). Summer 2018-2019. As a Teaching Assistant STATS 362 - Monte Carlo Methods. Winter 2019-2020. STATS 305 - Methods for Applied Statistics. Spring 2018-2019. STATS 200 - Introduction to Statistical Inference. Autumn 2018-2019, Winter 2017-2018, Winter 2016-2017, Autumn 2019-2020. STATS 237P - Theory of Investment Portfolios and Derivative Securities.

item.title: Talks
item.description: Knockoffs with side information. Banff workshop, March 2020. (Cancelled due to COVID-19) Derandomizing knockoffs: a stable multiple testing procedure. 54th Annual Conference on Information Sciences and Systems, March 2020. (Candelled due to COVID-19) Knockoffs with side information. Seminar on Statistics and Data Science, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, March 2020. Knockoffs with side information. Bernoulli-IMS One World Symposium, August 2020.

item.title: Research
item.description: Research interests Distribution-free inference Multiple hypothesis testing Data-driven decision making Causal inference Survival analysis Publications and Preprints Derandomized knockoffs: leveraging e-values for false discovery rate control [reproduction code] Zhimei Ren and Rina Foygel Barber Sensitivity analysis under the f-sensitivity models: definition, estimation and inference Ying Jin, Zhimei Ren* and Zhengyuan Zhou Runner up for Tom Ten Have Award at American Causal Inference Conference (ACIC) 2022 (presented by Ying Jin)
